pages.title: Brahmaputra Board
pages.text:
Chairman's Message
About Us / Head Of Organization /
Chairman's Message
Dr. Ranbir Singh, IAS (R)
Chairman, Brahmaputra Board
Brahmaputra Board was established by an Act of Parliament in 1980 to systematically address the problems of flood, erosion and congestion in the North Eastern (NE) region. The basins of Brahmaputra, Barak and other rivers of the NE region (including Sikkim and northern West Bengal) fall under the jurisdiction of Brahmaputra Board.
Our primary objective is to mitigate flood and erosion in the NE region. We aim to achieve this objective through integrated development of water resources for irrigation, hydropower, navigation and other beneficial uses, in active collaboration with the state governments and other partners, by using scientific methods and cutting-edge technologies. This approach promises water security, food security, livelihoods and economic development for the people of the NE region.
In pursuit of the aforesaid objective, we have already formulated masterplans for 52 sub-basins for implementation by the basin states. These masterplans have significantly helped the basin states to mitigate their flood, erosion and congestion problems by implementing the recommended projects. Such projects are eligible for 90% central funding under the Flood Management in Border Areas Program (FMBAP). We are currently formulating master plans for 12 more sub-basins and have also taken up the updation of the old master plans using new state-of-the-art technology in a phased manner.
The Board during its initial years took up the ambitious task of planning for mega-multipurpose projects on major tributaries like Subansiri, Siang, Dibang and Lohit etc to harness the hydropower potential of the rivers of the NE region, develop irrigation potential and mitigate flood and erosion. Some of these projects have already been implemented by the authorities concerned while others are under consideration.
The Board has made a huge contribution in protecting the inhabitants of the biggest river island of the world, i.e. Majuli from flood and erosion over the last 20 years by executing several projects. Satellite imagery bears testimony to the seminal success of this endeavor of the Brahmaputra Board. We have carried out anti-erosion works in almost all the basin states. We have also implemented several drainage development schemes to address the congestion in specific areas.
We have been working hard since 2004 to restore the Dibang and Lohit rivers to their original course to protect the people on the south bank of the Lohit in Dhola Hatigudi area. While we have successfully protected the area against erosion, the objective, of course restoration of these mighty rivers is yet to be achieved.
The Board in 2019 established its field offices in all 9 basin states to strengthen its engagement with the state governments. We also started innovative schemes like scientific reinforcement of indigenous water management practices, springshed management and nature-based solutions.
We are now making concerted efforts to transform Brahmaputra Board into a knowledge-based professional River Basin Organization (RBO), revitalize the North-East Hydraulic and Allied Research Institute (NEHARI), address human resource shortages, groom officers as subject matter experts, develop key institutional competencies in the technology and management domains, build robust partnerships, strengthen knowledge sharing among partners and stakeholders, and bring a strategic focus into the functioning of the Board. We are developing institutional capacity in the Board for providing technical assistance to the basin states towards implementing non-structural measures like flood forecasting, floodplain zoning, embankment asset management and sediment load management.
Our highly motivated team is working hard to take the Board to new heights. I wish them great success in their mission. I wish to assure all the basin states of high-quality technical assistance in the years to come. I express my gratitude to our Board members for their constructive ideas. I am deeply indebted to the High Power Review Board under the chairmanship of the Hon'ble Minister for Jal Shakti for their policy guidance and inspiration. Finally, I thank the Secretary, Water Resources, Government of India, for being the friend, philosopher and guide of the Board.

Coordination Office
Previous
Next
Coordination Office, Brahmaputra Board, Sector-10, Dwarka, New Delhi-75 typically handles tasks related to liaising with the Ministry of Jal Shakti. The role of Coordination Office includes:
Managing communication
: Ensuring that there is clear and consistent communication between our organization and the ministry.
Compliance and documentation
: Coordinating meetings and updates: Setting up meetings, providing updates, and ensuring that any requests or issues are addressed.
Problem-solving and support
: Ensuring that any regulatory requirements or formalities are completed and communicated properly.
Problem-solving and support
: Helping to resolve any issues that arise between our organization and the ministry, and ensuring smooth collaboration.

pages.title: Brahmaputra Board
pages.text:
About NEHARI
Laboratories
Research & Development
Training
Contact Details
Media Gallery
North Eastern Hydraulic and Allied Research Institute (NEHARI)
Offices /
North Eastern Hydraulic and Allied Research Institute (NEHARI)
Previous
Next
NEHARI, BRAHMAPUTRA BOARD
North Eastern region of India was not having adequate facility in the field of hydraulic model study, geo technical investigation and construction material testing related to river valley projects. These activities were earlier undertaken at the Central Water and Power Research Station (CWPRS), Pune in respect of hydraulic model experiments, and Central Soil and Material Research Station (CSMRS), New Delhi in respect of geo technical investigation and construction materials testing. With the establishment of the North Eastern Hydraulic and Allied Research Institute (NEHARI), a premier institute set up by the Brahmaputra Board, Ministry of Water Resources, Government of India, Ministry of Water Resources, River Development and Ganga Rejuvenation, as per the provisions under Clause 7 of the Assam Accord, at Guwahati, such studies can now effectively be undertaken in this Institute itself.
NEHARI is located at Rudreswar, at a distance of 25 km from the heart of the city of Guwahati on the North Bank of the river Brahmaputra, amidst scenic surroundings covering a total land of 44 ha. The hydraulic laboratory of NEHARI with 2 nos. of covered model trays is capable of carrying out river model studies pertaining to the rivers of the entire North Eastern Region having flood and erosion problems. On the other hand, construction materials like cement & aggregate, and foundation materials like rock & soil, in respect of various water resources projects in the entire North Eastern Region, can now be tested for their suitability and evaluation of other engineering parameters in the laboratories at NEHARI.
The institute started functioning from 1996 and continued its activities thereafter. However, there was a lull in activities for a brief period of time. NEHARI has been recently renovated in all respect including up-gradation of all laboratories (Soil, Concrete, Rock and Hydraulic) by way of installing upgraded version of all equipment. Renovated NEHARI was inaugurated by Hon’ble Union Minister of Jal Shakti, Government of India, Shri Gajendra Singh Shekhawat in 2021.
SCOPE OF ACTIVITY AT NEHARI
Laboratory Investigation
Hydraulic, Soil, Rock, and Concrete testing for planning and implementation of Water Resources Projects.
Research & Development
Work on basic and applied problems in River Engineering.
Consultancy Services
Development of anti-erosion, flood management, and other river engineering projects.
Training & Capacity Building
Programmes for Engineers in the Water Resources sector in the North Eastern Region.
Organogram of NEHARI

Regional Office - Sikkim
Offices / Sikkim Office /
Regional Office
Previous
Next
The Brahmaputra Board- Regional Office for Sikkim is located in Gangtok, Sikkim which functions to manage and oversee water resource projects in the basin. It is headed by the Superintending Engineer and is supported by the Gangtok Division, which is managed by the Executive Engineer. The office plays a crucial role in planning, executing, and monitoring projects aimed at flood control, erosion prevention, and sustainable water management.
The office is responsible for various technical activities, including the preparation of Detailed Project Reports (DPRs), execution of anti-erosion works, and promoting scientific research for improving water resource management. Special emphasis is placed on disseminating scientific knowledge and enhancing water management practices among the local tribes of the North Eastern Region. The office works closely with Water Resources & River Development Department, Government of Sikkim and other stakeholders to ensure effective implementation of projects.
Key projects and activities of the Regional Office, Sikkim, include Springshed management and catchment area development in Soreng and Namchi District in Sikkim and Preparation and updation of River Basin Master Plans.

The Brahmaputra Board- Regional Office for Arunachal Pradesh is located in Itanagar, functions to manage and oversee water resource projects in the basin. It is headed by the Deputy Chief Engineer and is supported by the Itanagar Division, which is managed by the Executive Engineer. The office plays a crucial role in planning, executing, and monitoring projects aimed at flood control, erosion prevention, and sustainable water management.
The office is responsible for a variety of technical activities, including the preparation of Detailed Project Reports (DPRs), execution of anti-erosion works, and promoting scientific research for improving water resource management. Special emphasis is placed on disseminating scientific knowledge and enhancing water management practices among the local tribes of the North Eastern Region. The office works closely with Water Resources Department, Government of Arunachal Pradesh and other stakeholders to ensure effective implementation of projects.
Key projects undertaken by the Regional Office, Arunachal Pradesh, include protection works at Mirem, Miglung, and Remi villages in East Siang District, and at Oyan and Sile villages, as well as sedimentation studies on the Siang and Dibang Basin in collaboration with IIT Guwahati. Other major initiatives/pilot projects include Water Management Practices of the Apatani-inhabited Ziro Valley and Pakke Valley, Springshed Management in Kombo village near Aalo town, and S&I and preparation of DPR for anti-erosion work in the Tissa River.

The Brahmaputra Board- Regional Office for Mizoram is located in Aizawl, functions to manage and oversee water resource projects in the basin. It is headed by the Superintending Engineer and is supported by the Aizawl Division, which is managed by the Executive Engineer. The office plays a crucial role in planning, executing, and monitoring projects aimed at flood control, erosion prevention, and sustainable water management.
The office is responsible for various technical activities, including the preparation of Detailed Project Reports (DPRs), execution of anti-erosion works, and promoting scientific research for improving water resource management. Special emphasis is placed on disseminating scientific knowledge and enhancing water management practices among the local tribes of the North Eastern Region. The office works closely with Water Resources Department, Government of Mizoram and other stakeholders to ensure effective implementation of projects.
Key projects/activities undertaken by the Regional Office, Aizawl, Mizoram, include the preparation of Detailed Project Reports (DPRs) for multiple water management and erosion control initiatives. These include the Springshed Management scheme for the lower reach of the Tlawng River, anti-erosion measures for the Tuiphai River in Tuiphai Valley, Serchhip District, and similar measures for the Tuipui River in Champhai District. Additionally, DPRs are being developed for anti-erosion interventions along the Chitelui River in Chitephai Valley, Vairengte, Kolasib District. The office also focuses on rejuvenating crucial water sources through the Dakla and Dialdawk Springshed projects in Mizoram and Preparation and updation of River Basin Master Plans.

Regional Office - Nagaland
Offices / Nagaland Office /
Regional Office
Previous
Next
The Brahmaputra Board- Regional Office for Nagaland is located in Dimapur, Nagaland which functions to manage and oversee water resource projects in the basin. It is headed by the Superintending Engineer and is supported by the Dimapur Division, which is managed by the Executive Engineer. The office plays a crucial role in planning, executing, and monitoring projects aimed at flood control, erosion prevention, and sustainable water management.
The office is responsible for various technical activities, including the preparation of Detailed Project Reports (DPRs), execution of anti-erosion works, and promoting scientific research for improving water resource management. Special emphasis is placed on disseminating scientific knowledge and enhancing water management practices among the local tribes of the North Eastern Region. The office works closely with Water Resources Department, Government of Nagaland and other stakeholders to ensure effective implementation of projects.
Key projects and activities of the Regional Office, Nagaland, focus on water resource management and sustainable development. These include Springshed rejuvenation in Ungma Village (Mokokchung District) and Pangti Village (Wokha District), as well as the Ahezu Spring at Natsumi Village (Zunheboto District). Additionally, the office is engaged in developing a three-tier farming system to enhance water resources in Chümoukedima and Peren Districts. Another key initiative is the implementation of drainage development for Dimapur Airport to improve water management and infrastructure resilience and Preparation and updation of River Basin Master Plans.

The Brahmaputra Board- Regional Office for Tripura is located in Agartala, which functions to manage and oversee water resource projects in the basin. It is headed by the Deputy Chief Engineer and is supported by the Agartala Division, which is managed by the Executive Engineer. The office plays a crucial role in planning, executing, and monitoring projects aimed at flood control, erosion prevention, and sustainable water management.
The office is responsible for various technical activities, including the preparation of Detailed Project Reports (DPRs), execution of anti-erosion works, and promoting scientific research for improving water resource management. Special emphasis is placed on disseminating scientific knowledge and enhancing water management practices among the local tribes of the North Eastern Region. The office works closely with Water Resources Department, Government of Tripura and other stakeholders to ensure effective implementation of projects.
Key projects undertaken by the Regional Office, Tripura, include Updation of Haora River Basin Master Plan, DPR preparation for Flood & Erosion Control of Khowai, Manu and Dhalai rivers.

pages.title: Brahmaputra Board
pages.text:
Introduction
About Us / Organisation /
Introduction
Brahmaputra Board is a statutory body set up under an Act of Parliament
(Brahmaputra Board Act 1980)
under the Ministry of Jal Shakti, Department of Water Resources, River Development & Ganga Rejuvenation. The jurisdiction of Brahmaputra Board includes Brahmaputra and  Barak Valley and covers all the States of the North Eastern Region including Sikkim and part  of West Bengal falling under Brahmaputra basin. The Board consists of 21 Members under the  Chairman, Brahmaputra Board (4 full time Members and 17 part time Members)
The Board started functioning with its Headquarters at Guwahati from 11.01.1982. As per subsection (2) of section (3) of the
Brahmaputra Board Rules, 1981
Board has established a Coordination Office at New Delhi.
The following are the main functions of Brahmaputra Board –
(1.)   To carry out survey and investigations in Brahmaputra Valley and to prepare Master Plan for the control of floods, bank erosion and improvement of drainage in the Brahmaputra Valley and activities connected therewith.
(2.)   To prepare the Master Plan in parts with reference to different areas of the Brahmaputra Valley or with reference to different matters relating to such areas and may as often as it considers necessary so to do, revise the Master Plan or any part thereof.
(3.)   To prepare Master Plan for development and utilization of water resources of the Brahmaputra Valley for irrigation, hydropower, navigation and other beneficial purposes.
(4.)   To submit Master Plan / revised Master Plan to the Central Government after consultation with the State Governments concerned and approve the same subject to such modifications as it may deem fit.
(5.)   To prepare detailed project report and estimates in respect of the dams and other projects in the Master Plan as approved by the Central Government and indicate in each case the cost attributable to different purposes or uses.
(6.)   To draw up standards and specifications for construction, operation and maintenance of such dams and other projects.
(7.)   To construct with the approval of the Central Government, multipurpose dams and works connected therewith as proposed in the Master Plan approved by the Central Govt. and maintain and operate such dams and works.
(8.)   To prepare in consultation with State Govt. concerned, a phased programme for construction by the State Government of all dams and other projects proposed in the Master Plan as approved by the Central Government other than those referred in above.
(9.)   The Board may maintain and operate any dam or project for so long as it deems necessary to do so.
(10.)  To perform such other functions as are supplemental, incidental or consequential to the functions specified above.
Organisational History and Set Up
Organisational History and Set Up
Brahmaputra Board is a statutory body, established under an Act of parliament
(Act 46 of 1980)
on December 31, 1981. The Act received presidential assent and was officially put into operation on December 31, 1981, as notified by the Central Government (G.S.R. 677(E) dated December 28, 1981, and published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, 1981, Part II, Section 3(i), Page 1960).
The Board's jurisdiction includes both the Brahmaputra and Barak Valleys and covers all the states of the North Eastern Region, including Sikkim and West Bengal. The Board consists of 21 members: 4 full-time members and 17 part-time members, representing seven states of the North Eastern Region, the North Eastern Council, concerned ministries (viz., Ministry of Jal Shakti, Agriculture, Finance, Power, and Surface Transport), and departments of the Government of India (viz., Central Water Commission, Geological Survey of India, India Meteorological Department, and the Central Electricity Authority).
After the extension of the Brahmaputra Board's jurisdiction in 2005 to include Sikkim and West Bengal, the Secretary, Irrigation & Waterways Department, Government of West Bengal, and the Secretary, WR & RDD, Government of Sikkim, Gangtok, Sikkim, are invited as special invitees. After the creation of the Ministry of DoNER, the Joint Secretary of DoNER is invited to Board meetings. The Advisor, NITI Aayog, is also invited as a special invitee. Further, the Ministry included the Joint Secretary (Administration), DoWR, RD&GR, as a special invitee.
The Board began functioning with its headquarters at Guwahati on January 11, 1982. As per sub-section (2) of section (3) of the Brahmaputra Board Rules, 1981, the Board has established a liaison office in New Delhi.
A
High Powered Review Board
to oversee the work of the Brahmaputra Board was constituted vide Resolution No. 2(17)/80/FC/460 dated March 19, 1982 (copy at Annexure-II) with the then Union Minister of Water Resources (now Jal Shakti) as the Chairman, and the Chief Ministers of  Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Nagaland, Sikkim, Tripura and West Bengal and the Union Minister/Ministers of State for Finance, Transport, Power, Agriculture, the Minister of State for Jal Shakti, the Secretary, Department of Water Resources, River Development and Ganga Rejuvenation, and the Chairman, Central Water Commission, as members. The Chairman of the Brahmaputra Board serves as the Member-Secretary, and the Member (RM), CWC, is a permanent invitee.
Mission and
Vision
Mission
To foster sustainable development and equitable utilization of the Brahmaputra River's water resources through effective regulation and development within the Inter-State Brahmaputra & Barak River Valley.
Vision
To achieve integrated flood and river basin management in the Northeast Region, encompassing both interstate and international rivers. This will be accomplished through collaborative efforts with state governments and key stakeholders, leveraging cutting-edge knowledge, technology, and the expertise of domain specialists.
Jurisdiction of Brahmaputra Board
Jurisdiction of Brahmaputra Board
The jurisdiction of Brahmaputra Board covers all the North Eastern States including Sikkim and northern part of West Bengal that drains into the Brahmaputra basin notified by Government of India vide
S.O. 2313 dated 18.12.2005.

on December 31, 1981. The Act received presidential assent and was officially put into operation on December 31, 1981, as notified by the Central Government (G.S.R. 677(E) dated December 28, 1981, and published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, 1981, Part II, Section 3(i), Page 1960).
The Board's jurisdiction includes both the Brahmaputra and Barak Valleys and covers all the states of the North Eastern Region, including Sikkim and West Bengal. The Board consists of 21 members: 4 full-time members and 17 part-time members, representing seven states of the North Eastern Region, the North Eastern Council, concerned ministries (viz., Ministry of Jal Shakti, Agriculture, Finance, Power, and Surface Transport), and departments of the Government of India (viz., Central Water Commission, Geological Survey of India, India Meteorological Department, and the Central Electricity Authority).
After the extension of the Brahmaputra Board's jurisdiction in 2005 to include Sikkim and West Bengal, the Secretary, Irrigation & Waterways Department, Government of West Bengal, and the Secretary, WR & RDD, Government of Sikkim, Gangtok, Sikkim, are invited as special invitees. After the creation of the Ministry of DoNER, the Joint Secretary of DoNER is invited to Board meetings. The Advisor, NITI Aayog, is also invited as a special invitee. Further, the Ministry included the Joint Secretary (Administration), DoWR, RD&GR, as a special invitee.
The Board began functioning with its headquarters at Guwahati on January 11, 1982. As per sub-section (2) of section (3) of the Brahmaputra Board Rules, 1981, the Board has established a liaison office in New Delhi.
A High Powered Review Board to oversee the work of the Brahmaputra Board was constituted vide Resolution No. 2(17)/80/FC/460 dated March 19, 1982 (copy at Annexure-II) with the then Union Minister of Water Resources (now Jal Shakti) as the Chairman, and the Chief Ministers of  Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Nagaland, Sikkim, Tripura and West Bengal and the Union Minister/Ministers of State for Finance, Transport, Power, Agriculture, the Minister of State for Jal Shakti, the Secretary, Department of Water Resources, River Development and Ganga Rejuvenation, and the Chairman, Central Water Commission, as members. The Chairman of the Brahmaputra Board serves as the Member-Secretary, and the Member (RM), CWC, is a permanent invitee.

pages.title: Brahmaputra Board
pages.text:
Frequently Asked Questions
Frequently Asked Questions
1
What is the Brahmaputra Board ?
+
Brahmaputra Board is a statutory body under the Ministry of Jal Shakti,  Department of Water Resources, Government of India. It is responsible for planning and implementing integrated measures for flood control and  riverbank erosion in the North Eastern Region, including Sikkim and the northern part of West Bengal.
2
When was the Brahmaputra Board set up ?
+
The Brahmaputra Board was established in January 1980.
3
What is the main purpose of the Brahmaputra Board ?
+
The primary goal of the Brahmaputra Board is to manage and control floods and riverbank erosion through scientific planning and holistic river basin management practices.
4
Where is the Brahmaputra Board headquarters located ?
+
The headquarters is located in Guwahati, Assam, India.
5
Which states fall under the jurisdiction of the Brahmaputra Board ?
+
The Board's jurisdiction covers Assam, Arunachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, Manipur, Mizoram, Nagaland, Tripura, Sikkim, and the northern part of West Bengal.
6
Does the Brahmaputra Board deal exclusively with the Brahmaputra River ?
+
No.The Board jurisdiction cover three major basins:
Brahmaputra Basin
Barak Basin
Other Minor Basins
Therefore, the Board's jurisdiction covers all the North Eastern States of India, namely Assam, Arunachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, Manipur, Mizoram, Nagaland, Tripura, Sikkim, and the northern part of West Bengal.
7
What are the main activities of the Brahmaputra Board ?
+
The primary activity is comprehensive planning through River Basin Master Plans, which form the foundation for developing Detailed Project Reports (DPRs).
Detailed Project Reports (DPRs) of Projects related to flood management,riverbank protection, and water resource development are implemented based on these plans.
The Master Plans are periodically updated to address evolving needs and priorities.
8
Does the Brahmaputra Board conduct research and capacity building ?
+
Yes. The Board actively engages in research and capacity building through the North Eastern Hydraulic and Allied Research Institute (NEHARI).
9
How does the Brahmaputra Board help in flood control and riverbank erosion ?
+
The Board prepares Master Plans for each river in the NE States. Based on recommendations from these plans, various activities such as detailed scientific research and projects on flood control, anti-erosion measures, and watershed conservation are undertaken by the State/Central Government/Stakeholders .
10
How many River Basin Master Plans have been prepared ?
+
A total of 73 Master Plans have been identified, of which 52 have been  prepared and approved by the Government of India. The Master Plans are continuously updated. For the latest updates, please visit the publication page on the official website.
11
How can I access the Master Plans?
+
Submit a formal request to the Secretary, Brahmaputra Board, at secy-bbrd@gov.in, bbrd-ghy@nic.in. Upon approval, access will be provided at the Library of the Brahmaputra Board (HQ) in Basistha, Guwahati, Assam.
12
Does the Brahmaputra Board undertake specific projects ?
+
The Board supports State Governments by providing technical expertise. Projects identified in the Master Plans are undertaken upon need assessment/request by various Departments/Stakeholders and subject to the approval of the Central Government.
13
How can I find information about ongoing projects ?
+
Visit the official website of the
Brahmaputra Board
.
14
What is NEHARI ?
+
The North Eastern Hydraulic and Allied Research Institute (NEHARI) is a premier research institute under the Brahmaputra Board. It provides technical support and capacity development on hydraulic, hydrological, geotechnical, and allied aspects related to water resources management in the North Eastern region.
15
What are NEHARI's key functions ?
+
Conducting hydraulic model studies on river behaviour and flood management.
Providing geotechnical investigations for embankments, dams, and riverbank protection.
Offering training programs and capacity-building workshops.
Supporting stakeholders, including PSUs, private companies, and research institutions.
16
Where is NEHARI located ?
+
NEHARI is located in Rudreshwar, North Guwahati, Assam.
17
Who can access NEHARI's services ?
+
NEHARI's services are available to State/Central Government Departments, PSUs, private companies, research institutions, and citizens involved in water resource management in the North Eastern region.
18
How does NEHARI contribute to flood management ?
+
NEHARI conducts hydraulic model studies to understand river behavior, assesses the effectiveness of flood control structures, and offers technical recommendations for embankments and river training works. It also provides training and capacity-building programs for engineers and officers.
19
How can I contact the Brahmaputra Board ?
+
Contact details, including email addresses, are available in the
"Contact Us"
20
Can individuals contact the Board with concerns ?
+
Yes, individuals can reach out through the
"Contact Us"
information provided on the official website.
21
Does the Brahmaputra Board have offices in all North Eastern States ?
+
Yes, it has offices in each state, each headed by a Regional Officer as below:

The jurisdiction of Brahmaputra Board covers all the North Eastern States including Sikkim and northern part of West Bengal that drains into the Brahmaputra basin notified by Government of India vide

pages.title: Brahmaputra Board
pages.text:
Acts & Legislation
Others / Documents /
Acts
THE BRAHMAPUTRA BOARD ACT, 1980   NO. 46 OF 1980
An Act to provide for the establishment of a Board for the planning  implementation of measures for the control of flood and bank erosion in Brahmaputra Valley  and for matters connected therewith.  Be it enacted by Parliament in the Thirty-first year of the Republic of India as   follows:
Download PDF
CHAPTER - I PRELIMINARY
Short title and commencement.
1. (1) This Act may be called the Brahmaputra Board Act, 1980.
(2) It shall come into force on such date as the Central Government may, by notification in the Official Gazette, appoint.
Declaration as to expediency of Control by the Union
2. It is hereby declared that it is expedient in the public interest that the Central Government should take under its control the regulation and development of the inter-State Brahmaputra river valley to the extent hereinafter provided.
Definition
3. In this Act, unless the context otherwise requires:-
a) "Board" means the Brahmaputra Board established under section 4.
b) "Brahmaputra Valley" means the inter-State Brahmaputra river valley as demarcated under section 11.
c) "Fund" means the Brahmaputra Board Fund constituted under section 19.
d) "Master Plan" means the Master Plan for the control of floods, bank erosion, and improvement of drainage in the Brahmaputra Valley prepared under section 12, and includes, where it is prepared in parts, each such part.
e) "Member" means a member of the Board.
f) "Prescribed" means prescribed by rules made under this Act.
g) "Regulations" means regulations made by the Board under this Act.
h) "Rule" means rules made by the Central Government under this Act.
i) "State Government", in relation to a Union Territory, means the administrator thereof appointed under article 239 of the Constitution.
CHAPTER-II ESTABLISHMENT OF THE BOARD
Establishment and Incorporation of the Brahmaputra Board
4. (1) With effect from such date as the Central Government may, by notification in the Official Gazette, appoint, there shall be established for the purposes of this Act a Board, to be called the Brahmaputra Board.
(2) The Board shall be a body corporate by the name aforesaid, having perpetual succession and a common seal with power, subject to the provisions of this Act, to acquire, hold, and dispose of property, both movable and immovable, and to contract and shall by the said name sue and be sued.
The Board shall consist of the following members:
(a) A Chairman and a Vice-Chairman to be appointed by the Central Government.
(b) The General Manager of the Board and the Financial Adviser to the Board, ex officio.
(c) A member each to represent respectively the Governments of Assam, Meghalaya, Nagaland, Manipur, and Tripura, and the Administrations of Arunachal Pradesh and Mizoram, and the North-Eastern Council constituted under section 3 of the North-Eastern Council Act, 1971, to be appointed by the Central Government.
(d) A member each to represent respectively the Ministries of the Central Government dealing with agriculture, irrigation, finance, power, and transport, to be appointed by the Central Government.
(e) A member each to represent respectively the Central Water Commission, the Central Electricity Authority, the Geological Survey of India, and the India Meteorological Department, to be appointed by the Central Government.
(4) If any member, for infirmity or otherwise, is incapable of carrying out his duties or is absent on leave otherwise than in circumstances not involving the vacation of his appointment, the Central Government may appoint another person to act in his place.
(5) Any officer of the Central Government, not being a member of the Board, if deputed by the Government in this behalf, shall have the right to attend the meetings of the Board and take part in the proceedings thereof, but shall not be entitled to vote.
(6) The Board may associate with itself, in such manner and for such purposes as may be determined by regulations, any person whose assistance or advice it may desire in complying with any of the provisions of this Act. A person so associated shall have the right to take part in the discussions of the Board relevant to the purpose for which he has been associated, but shall not be entitled to vote.
No act or proceeding of the Board shall be invalidated merely by reason of:
(a) Any vacancy in, or any defect in the constitution of, the Board.
(b) Any defect in the appointment of the person acting as a member of the Board.
(c) Any irregularity in the procedure of the Board not affecting the merits of the case.
(8) Subject to any rules made under this Act, the Board may constitute a Standing Committee consisting of the General Manager of the Board, Financial Adviser to the Board, and three other members of the Board.
(9) The Standing Committee shall perform, exercise, and discharge such of the functions, powers, and duties of the Board as may be prescribed or as may be delegated to it by the Board.
Conditions of service of members.
5. The term of office of the members (other than the ex officio members) and other conditions of service of the members shall be such as may be prescribed.
Powers of Chairman and Vice-Chairman
6 (1) The Chairman shall, in addition to presiding over the meetings of the Board, exercise and discharge such powers and duties of the Board as may be delegated to him by the Board and such other powers and duties as may be prescribed.
(2) The Vice-Chairman of the Board shall exercise and discharge such of the powers and duties of the Chairman as may be prescribed or as may be delegated to him by the Chairman.
General Manager
7. (1) The Central Government shall appoint the General Manager of the Board.
(2) The terms and conditions of service of the General Manager shall be such as may be prescribed.
(3) Subject to the general superintendence and control of the Board and the Chairman of the Board, the General Manager shall be the Chief Executive Authority of the Board.
(4) The General Manager shall exercise and discharge such of the powers and duties of the Chairman as may be prescribed or, as may be delegated to him by the Chairman, and such other powers and duties as may be prescribed by rules or determined by regulations.
Financial Adviser
8. (1) The Central Government shall appoint the Financial Adviser to the Board.
(2) The terms and conditions of service of the Financial Adviser shall be such as may be prescribed.
Chief Engineers, Secretary and other officers
9. (1) The Central Government shall appoint -
(a) two Chief Engineers to assist the General Manager of the Board;
(b) the Secretary to the Board;
(2) The Board may appoint such other officers and employees as it considers necessary for the efficient discharge of its functions under this Act.
(3) The terms and conditions of service of the Chief Engineers, the Secretary and other officers and employees of the Board shall be such as may be determined by regulations.
Advisory Committees.
10. Subject to any rules made in this behalf, the Board may from time to time constitute such Advisory Committees as may be necessary for the efficient discharge of its functions.
CHAPTER-III FUNCTIONS AND POWER OF THE BOARD
Limits of the Brahmaputra Valley
11. (1) As soon as may be after the commencement of this Act, the Central Government shall by notification in the Official Gazette demarcate the limits of the Brahmaputra Valley for the purpose of this Act.
(2) The Board shall perform such of its functions and exercise such of the powers within such areas in the Brahmaputra Valley as the Central Government may, by notification in the Official Gazette specify from time to time. Provided that before issuing any notification in respect of any area under this sub-section, the Central Government shall consult the Government of the State within which such area is situated.
Master Plan for the Control of Floods etc. in the Brahmaputra Valley
12. (1) Subject to the other provisions of this Act and the rules, the Board shall carry out surveys and investigations in the Brahmaputra Valley and prepare a Master Plan for the control of floods and bank erosion and improvement of drainage in the Brahmaputra Valley: Provided that the Board may prepare the Master Plan in part with reference to different areas of the Brahmaputra Valley or with reference to different matters relating to such areas and may as often as it considers necessary so to do, revise the Master Plan or any part thereof.
(2) In preparing the Master Plan, the Board shall have regard to the development and utilization of water resources of the Brahmaputra Valley for irrigation, hydro power, navigation and other beneficial purposes and shall, as far as possible, indicate in such plan the works and other measures which may be undertaken for such development.
(3) The Master Plan shall be submitted to the Central Government as soon as may be after it has been prepared or, as the case may be revised and the Central Government shall, after consultation with the State Governments concerned, approve the same subject to such modifications as it may deem fit.
Other functions of the Board
13. (1) The Board shall also -
(a) Prepare detailed reports and estimates in respect of the dams and other projects proposed in the Master Plan as approved by the Central Government and indicate in each case the cost attributable to different purposes or uses;
(b) Draw up standards and specifications for the construction, operation and maintenance of such dams and other projects;
(c) Construct, with the approval of the Central Government, multipurpose dams and works connected therewith proposed in the Master Plan as approved by the Central Government and maintain and operate such dams and works;
(d) Prepare, in consultation with the State Governments concerned, a phased programme for the construction by the State Government of all dams and other projects proposed in the Master Plan as approved by the Central Government other than those referred to in clause (c);
(e) Perform any other function which may be prescribed for the proper implementation of this Act.
(f) Perform such other functions as are supplemental, incidental or consequential to the functions specified in section 12 or in clauses (a) to (d), or prescribed under clause (c), of this sub-section.
(2) Notwithstanding anything contained in clause (d) of sub section (1), the Board may, with the prior approval of the Central Government, construct any dam or project referred to in that clause if it is satisfied, having regard to the cost of construction, and the expertise required for the construction, of any such dam or project, that it is expedient so to do.
(3) The Board may maintain and operate any dam or project referred to in sub-section (2) for so long as it deems if necessary so to do.
Explanation - For the purposes of this section, "multipurpose dam" means a dam which is constructed for purpose of flood control and for other purposes.
Conditions subject to which the Board may perform functions
14. The performance by the Board of the functions specified in or prescribed under, section 12 and 13 shall be subject to the following conditions, namely:
(a) No multipurpose dam referred to in clause (c) or sub-section (1) of section 13 shall be constructed by the Board unless the State Government concerned make available the land required for the purpose;
(b) No dam or project referred to in clause (d) of sub-section (1) of section 13 shall be constructed by the Board unless the State Governments concerned make available free of cost the land required for its execution and also undertake to take over its maintenance on and from the expiry of such period after its completion as may be specified by the Board;
(c) No dam or other works shall undertaken by the Board unless the State Government concerned agree to provide all such assistance as may be required for the construction, operation and maintenance thereof;
(d) Such other conditions (including conditions relating to the sharing by the State Government concerned of the whole or any part of cost of dam or other works constructed by the Board) as may be specified by the Central Government by general or special order published in the Official Gazette;
Provided that before undertaking the construction of any such dam or other works, the Board shall apprise the State Governments concerned of the cost of construction of, and the benefits likely to accrue from, such dam or other works and the proportion in which the State Governments shall share such cost and benefits;
Provided further that if the Board the State Governments are unable to agree in respect of the sharing of the cost and benefits of any such dam or other works and Board shall refer the matter to the Central Government for decision and the Central Government shall decide such matter after consulting the State Governments and the decision of the Central Government shall be final.
General powers of the Board
15. (1) Subject to the provision of this Act and the rules, the Board shall have the power to do anything which may be necessary or expedient for the purpose of performing its functions under this Act.
(2) Without prejudice to the generality of the provisions of sub-section (1) the Board may
(a) Publish statistics or other information relating to various aspects of flood control, bank erosion and drainage in the Brahmaputra valley.
(b) Require the State Governments concerned to furnish to it information relating to the measures undertaken by them for the control of floods and bank erosion and improvement of drainage in the Brahmaputra valley, topographical, meteorological and hydrological and other related data and such other information as the Board may require for the performance of its functions under this Act.
Forwarding of, and consultation with respect to plans etc., prepared by the Board
16. (1) The Board shall forward copies of the Master Plan, reports, estimates, standards and specifications prepared by it to the Central Government and State Government concerned.
(2) The Central Government and the State Government concerned may consult the Board with regard to any matters connected with, or arising out of, such plan, reports estimates or standards and specifications.
16. (3) If, for any reason, a State Government considers it necessary to execute any project for the control of floods and bank erosion and drainage work in the Brahmaputra Valley and such project is not envisaged in the Master Plan or such project is intended to be executed by the State Government in a manner not in conformity with the Master Plan, the State Government may consult the Board with regard to the execution of the project and the Board may make such recommendations as it may deem fit: Provided that nothing containing in this sub-section shall be construed as imposing any requirement on any State Government to consult the Board with regard to the execution of any works which have become urgently necessary by reason of any emergency or other extraordinary circumstances.
CHAPTER-IV CONTROL BY CENTRAL GOVERNMENT
Directions and instructions by Central Government.
17. (1) The Central Government may, from time to time, issue to the Board
such directions and instruction as it may deem necessary for the efficient
administration of this Act and the Board shall carry out such directions and instructions.
(2) In particular and without prejudice to the generality of the provisions of subsection (1), the Central Government may, while granting (whether directly or
though the Board and after due appropriation made by Parliament by law in that
behalf) any financial assistance, by way of loans or grants for the execution by
any State Government of any dam or other project referred to in clause (d) of
sub-section (1) of section 13 after obtaining the approval of the State
Government in this behalf, direct the Board to exercise all such powers as may
be necessary for satisfying itself that the works are being executed in
accordance with the standards and specifications laid down therefor by the
Board.
CHAPTER-V FINANCE, ACCOUNTS AND AUDIT
Grants and loans by Central Government.
18. The Central Government may, after due appropriation made by Parliament by law in this behalf, pay to the Board such sums of money as the Central Government may consider necessary.
Construction of Brahmaputra Board Fund
19. (1) There shall be constituted a fund to be called the Brahmaputra Board Fund and there shall be credited thereto the sums paid to the Board by the Central Government or by any State Government and all other sums received by the Board.
(2) The Fund shall be applied:
(a) For meeting the salary, allowances and other remuneration of the members, officers and other employees of the Board and other administrative expenses of the Board;
(b) For meeting the expenditure on surveys and investigations undertaken by the Board;
(c) For meeting the cost of construction, operation and maintenance of projects undertaken by the Board;
(d) For meeting the other expenses of the Board in the discharge of its functions under this Act;
(e) If any sums are received by the Board under sub-section (2) of section 17, for the payment of such sums to the State Governments concerned.
Budget
20. The Board shall prepare, in such form and at such time each year as may be prescribed, its budget for the next financial year, showing the estimated expenditure, the amount of expenditure which any State Government has undertaken to provide for, and forward the same to the Central Government.
Annual report
21. The Board shall prepare, in such form and at such time each year as may be prescribed, its annual report, giving a full account of its activities during the previous year, and forward copies thereof to the Central Government and the Government shall cause the same to be laid before each House of Parliament.
Accounts and Audit
22. The accounts of the Board shall be maintained and audited in such manner as may, in consultation with the Comptroller and Auditor General of India, be prescribed.
CHAPTER-VI MISCELLANEOUS
Removal etc. of members
24. (1) The Central Government may remove from the Board any member who, in its opinion, -
(a) refuses to act,
(b) has become incapable to act,
(c) has so abused his office as a member so as to render his continuance on the Board detrimental to the interests of the public, or
(d) is otherwise unsuitable to continue as a member,
(2) The Central Government may suspend any member pending an inquiry against him.
(3) No order of removal under this section shall be made unless the member concerned has been given an opportunity to submit his explanation.
(4) A member who has been removed shall not be eligible for reappointment under the Board.
(5) If the Board fails to carry out its functions or directions, the Central Government may reconstitute the Board.
Power to enter
25. Subject to rules, any officer authorized by the Board may enter land or premises to carry out works, surveys, or investigations. Provided no entry to private dwellings without 7-day prior notice and occupier consent.
Members, officers and employees of the Board to be Public servants
26. All members, officers, and employees of the Board shall be deemed to be public servants under section 21 of the Indian Penal Code.
Protection of action taken in good faith
(1) No legal proceedings shall lie against the Government or Board members for anything done in good faith under this Act.
(2) No suit shall lie against the Board for flood damages or failures of works done in good faith.
Power to make rules
(1) The Central Government may, by notification, make rules to carry out this Act.
(2) Such rules may cover matters including standing committees, service terms, duties, budgeting, auditing, arbitration process, and others.
Power to make regulations
(1) The Board may, with prior Central Government approval, make regulations to implement this Act.
(2) Such regulations may cover association with persons, General Manager duties, service conditions, and any other relevant matter.
Rules and regulations to be laid before Parliament
30. All rules and regulations must be laid before both Houses of Parliament for 30 days. If both Houses agree on modifications or annulment, such changes take effect accordingly.
Disputes between the Board and the State Government
32. (1) Disputes between the Board and State Governments shall be referred to the Central Government.
(2) The Central Government shall attempt resolution via negotiation or conciliation.
(3) If unresolved, the Central Government may refer the dispute to arbitration by a person appointed by the Chief Justice of India.
(4) Arbitrator may appoint assessors.
(5) Arbitrator’s decision is final and binding.
(6) Arbitration Act, 1940 shall not apply.

pages.title: Brahmaputra Board
pages.text:
Members of HPRB
About Us / Members /
Members of HPRB
High Powered Review Board:
A High Powered Review Board to oversee the work of the Brahmaputra Board was constituted with the Union Minister of the then Water Resources (Now MoJS) as the Chairman; Chief Minister of Assam, Arunachal Pradesh, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Nagaland & Tripura; Union Minister/Ministers of State for Finance, Transport, Power, Agriculture, the then Water Resources (Now MoJS); Secretary, MoJS, DoWR, RD&GR; Chairman, Central Water Commission as Members and Chairman, Brahmaputra Board as the Member-Secretary. Member (RM), CWC is a permanent invitee.
Resolution
The parliament has enacted an Act in September 1980 for constituting the Brahmaputra Board to prepare a Master Plan for control of floods and bank erosion and improvement of drainage in the Brahmaputra Valley keeping regard to the development and utilisation of the water resources of the Brahmaputra Valley for irrigation, hydropower, navigation and other beneficial purposes. The Board may also take up construction of the multi-purpose projects as approved by the Central Government. The Brahmaputra Board, an autonomous Statutory Body, under the Ministry of Irrigation, has been constituted with effect from 31st December, 1981.
It has been decided to constitute a high-powered Review Board to oversee the work of the Statutory Board constituted in pursuance of the Brahmaputra Board Act of 1980, consisting of the following:
Sl. No.
Member
Photo
Designation

Hon'ble Union Minister for Jal Shakti

Member

Chairman
Member (Floods), Central Water Commission, will be a permanent invitee to the meetings of the Board.
The Board will lay down board policies and oversee the work of the Brahmaputra Board for its efficient functioning.
The Board will frame its own Rules of Business. The head- quarters of the Board will be Gauhati.
The Secretariat assistance to the Board will be "provided by the Chairman of the Brahmaputra Board, Gauhati.

pages.title: Brahmaputra Board
pages.text:
Standing Committee Members
About Us / Members /
Standing Committee Members
G.S.R. 112 - In exercise of the powers conferred by sub-section (2) of Section 28 of the Brahmaputra Board Act, 1980 (46 of 1980), the Central Government hereby makes the following rules, namely :-
Short title and commencement
1. These rules may be called the Brahmaputra Board (Standing Committee Rules, 1996)
2. They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette.
Definition - In these rules, unless the context otherwise requires :-
(a) “Act” means the Brahmaputra Board Act, 1980 (46 of 1980)
(b) “Board” means the Brahmaputra Board established under Section 4 of the Act.
(c) “Master Plans” means Master Plans prepared by the Brahmaputra Board.
(d) “Chairman” means the Chairman of the Brahmaputra Board.
(e) “Vice-Chairman” means the Vice-Chairman of the Brahmaputra Board
(f) “General Manager” means the General Manager of the Board.
(g) “Financial Adviser” means the Financial Adviser to the Board.
(h) “Member” means Member, Central Water Commission.
(i) “Secretary to the Government of Assam” means the Secretary to the Government of Assam, Flood Control Department.
3. All words and expressions used in these rules but not defined shall have the meaning respectively assigned to them in the Act.
4. Composition of the Standing Committee:- Members of Standing Committee of Brahmaputra Board:
Sl. No.
Member
Designation
1.
Chairman /
Vice Chairman,
Brahmaputra Board, Basistha, Guwahati29.
Chairman
2.
Member (RM), Central Water Commission, Sewa Bhawan, R.K.
Puram, New Delhi–110066
Member
3.

Basistha, Guwahati29.

4.

Guwahati, Assam
Member
5.

Basistha, Guwahati-29.
Member (Convener)
Invitee of Standing Committee of Brahmaputra Board
Sl. No.
Member
1.
Chief Engineer, Brahmaputra Basin Organization (BBO), Central
Water Commission, Adabari, Guwahati, Assam.
2.
Chief Engineer, Barak & Other Basin Organization (B&OBO),
Central
Water Commission, Shillong, Meghalaya.
3.
Director (M&A), Central Water Commission, Adabari, Guwahati-14.
4.
Regional Director, North Eastern Region, Central Ground Water
Board (CGWB), Department of Water Resources, RD&GR, Govt. of
India, Betkuchi, Guwahati-781035
5.
Regional Head, National Institute of Hydrology, North Eastern
Regional Centre (NERC), Khanapara, Guwahati-781022.
5. Duties, Powers, and Functions of the Standing Committee:- Members of Standing Committee of Brahmaputra Board:
(a) Examine the annual Action Plan of the proposed survey and investigations prepared by the Board vis-a-vis budget allocation for every financial year and advise the Board to take action appropriately.
(b) Advise the Board on various investigations and studies to be taken up for the preparation of master plans, multipurpose projects, drainage development schemes and other works as decided by the Board.
(c) Decide the priority works to be taken up by the Brahmaputra Board under the framework of the Board.
(d) Indicate a time frame for the studies, investigations, and works mentioned in (b) and (c)
(e) Decisions/approval of the Standing Committee shall be placed before the Board in its immediate next meeting for its ratification.
(f) exercise financial powers and delegate financial powers to the various executive Functionaries in the field offices of the Board for carrying out their day-to-day activity.

Upper Brahmaputra Circle Office
Offices / Upper Brahmaputra Circle Office /
Regional Office
Previous
Next
Upper Brahmaputra Circle, Brahmaputra Board, Jorhat looks after the works carried out by    Brahmaputra Board in the Upper Assam Region. The Circle also coordinates with the stake holders of State Departments and other Government of India agencies in planning and integrated  implementations of flood control and anti-erosion projects on the banks of Brahmaputra in the Upper Assam region. The Circle has 3(three) Divisions under its jurisdiction namely-.
Each division is headed by an Executive Engineer and plays a crucial role in the successful execution of various projects and initiatives.
Key Initiatives and Projects
The Circle is responsible for executing several significant river basin management schemes, which include:
Protection of Majuli Island: Safeguarding one of the world's largest river islands from the impact of erosion and environmental degradation.
Bio-Engineering Measures: Implementing eco-friendly engineering solutions to control erosion and protect river banks.
Restoration of the Dholla-Hatighuli Course: Efforts are underway to restore the Dholla-Hatighuli River to its original course, ensuring ecological balance and better flood management.
Survey investigations and preparing the Detailed Project Report (DPR) for the Jiadhal Dam Project, a pivotal infrastructure project aimed at enhancing water management, irrigation, and flood control in the region.
Through these strategic initiatives, the Upper Brahmaputra Circle remains committed to the sustainable management of the Brahmaputra Basin, protecting its natural resources, and ensuring the safety and well-being of the communities that depend on them.

Lower Brahmaputra Circle Office
Offices / Lower Brahmaputra Circle Office /
Regional Office
Previous
Next
The Brahmaputra Board Lower Brahmaputra Circle is located in Nalbari District, Assam and functions to manage and oversee water resource projects in the basin. It is headed by the Superintending Engineer and is supported by the Nalbari Division, which is managed by the Executive Engineer. The office plays a crucial role in planning, executing, and monitoring projects aimed at flood control, erosion prevention, and sustainable water management.
The office is responsible for a variety of technical activities, including the preparation of Detailed Project Reports (DPRs), execution of anti-erosion works, and promoting scientific research for improving water resource management. Special emphasis is placed on disseminating scientific knowledge and enhancing water management practices among the local tribes of the North Eastern Region. The office works closely with Water Resources Department, Government of Assam and other stakeholders to ensure effective implementation of projects.
Key projects/activities undertaken by the Lower Brahmaputra Circle, include consultancy for a Detailed Project Report to mitigate flash floods and erosion in BTC by rivers like Pagla, Aie, Beki, and Sankosh. It also focuses on the Dong Water Management scheme to enhance traditional water practices among indigenous communities, protection of Kalair Alga from Brahmaputra erosion, Barbhag Drainage Development Scheme and the Pagladiya Dam Project for flood control, irrigation, and water resource management in the region and Preparation and updation of River Basin Master Plans.

The Barak Valley Circle of Brahmaputra Board is located in Silchar, Assam which functions to manage and oversee water resource projects in the barak basin. It is headed by the Superintending Engineer and is supported by the Silchar Division, which is managed by the Executive Engineer. The office plays a crucial role in planning, executing, and monitoring projects aimed at flood control, erosion prevention, and sustainable water management.
The office is responsible for various technical activities, including the preparation of Detailed Project Reports (DPRs), execution of anti-erosion works, and promoting scientific research for improving water resource management. The office works closely with Water Resources Department, Government of Assam and other stakeholders to ensure effective implementation of projects/schemes.
Key projects/activities undertaken by the Barak Valley Circle, Assam, include DPR for construction of Amjur DDS at Sonai, Cachar and Study on Flood and Soil Erosion of Barak River with NIT-Silchar and Preparation and updation of River Basin Master Plans.

Vigilance Office
Vigilance /
Vigilance Office
The Vigilance Wing of Brahmaputra Board constitutes one Junior Engineer, one LDC and one MTS headed by the Chief Vigilance Officer and the Vigilance Officer
The main objective of this wing is to eliminate corruption and prevent any form of misconduct by adhering to morality and ethics upholding the santity of the Board
Shri Vikhosa Kezo
Chief Vigilance Officer,
Brahmaputra Board, Basistha, Guwahati, Assam-781029
Tel No: ****-******
Email: vigilance-bbrd[at]gov[dot]in
Shri Shyamal Kumar Deka
Vigilance Officer,
Brahmaputra Board, Basistha, Guwahati, Assam-781029
Tel No: 0361-2302868
Email: vigilance-bbrd[at]gov[dot]in

1
Opening Ceremony of Vigilance Awareness Week
Integrity Pledge taken by all the members of Brahmaputra Board with a list of agendas.
50
28.10.2024
2
Debate Competition for Board Employees
A debate was organized among the newly appointed board members on the topic Culture of Integrity for Nation’s Prosperity.
10
3
Workshop (Hybrid Mode) by the Members of Competition Commission of India
Members from Competition Commission of India gave an overview of the commission with their valuable speech.
50
29.10.2024
4
Speech/Presentation on Systems and Procedures of Organisation – Sri N. Pegu (Deputy Secretary, Brahmaputra Board)
The Deputy Secretary of Brahmaputra Board, Shri Nirmal Pegu, gave a significant speech on Central Conduct Rules to the newly appointed members of the Board.
15
5
Speech/Presentation on Ethics & Governance and Vigilance Awareness – Dr. Nanditesh Nilay
Dr. Nanditesh Nilay gave an overview on Vigilance Awareness and also on Ethics and Governance.
50
30.10.2024
6
Closing Ceremony of Vigilance Awareness Week
The program ended with new resolutions for the betterment of the Board.
50
04.11.2024
7
A Symposium on Cyber Hygiene, Security, and other IT-related matters
A symposium was held regarding Cyber Security to educate the Board members about the risks related to online platforms and also informed them about the remedies.
50
05.11.2024

Director's Office
The Brahmaputra Board is dedicated to implementing key river basin management projects and promoting sustainable development across the region. The Circle Office is located in Rudreswar, North Guwahati-781030, Assam. The Director's office is led by the Director, supported by Assistant Executive Engineer.

pages.title: Brahmaputra Board
pages.text:
About NEHARI
Laboratories
Research & Development
Training
Contact Details
Media Gallery
North Eastern Hydraulic and Allied Research Institute (NEHARI)
Offices /
North Eastern Hydraulic and Allied Research Institute (NEHARI)
Previous
Next
AVAILABLE LABORATORIES AT NEHARI
HYDRALUICS LABORATORY
SOIL MECHANICS LABORATORY
CONSTRUCTION MATERIALS TESTING LABORATORY
ROCK MECHANICS LABORATORY
HYDRALUICS LABORATORY
The hydraulic laboratory has a water conductor system capable of  re-circulation 1.24cumes (40 cusec) of water to feed two nos. of covered model  trays. Each  model has a capacity to accommodate a stretch of 50 km reach of a prototype for conducting hydraulic experiments.
Electronic Velocity Meter
An Electronic Velocity Meter measures fluid speed (e.g.,
water or air) using electronic sensors like electromagnetic or ultrasonic technology. Compact and  portable, it offers accurate, real-time readings (e.g., 0.1 to 6 m/s) with no moving parts, ideal for hydrology,  environmental monitoring, and engineering tasks like stream flow or flood analysis.
Acoustic Doppler Velocity Meter
An Acoustic Doppler Velocity Meter (ADVM) measures water  velocity using the Doppler effect of sound waves  reflected off particles. It provides precise, real-time data (e.g., 0.01 to 5 m/s) with no moving parts, perfect for hydrology and stream monitoring in rivers and canals.
UAV
An Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV), or drone, is a remotely  piloted or autonomous aircraft used for tasks like  surveillance, mapping, or data collection. It’s compact, versatile, and equipped with sensors or cameras, ideal for applications in agriculture, defense, and  environmental monitoring.
Automatic Water Level Recorder
An Automatic Water Level Recorder (AWLR) is a device that continuously monitors and records water levels in rivers, reservoirs, or wells using sensors like pressure transducers or ultrasonic waves. It provides real-time data, is low-maintenance, and is widely used in hydrology and flood management.
SOIL MECHANICS LABORATORY
Triaxial Test, Direct Shear Test, Mechanical Analysis (grain size), Standard  Proctor Compaction Test, Atterbergs Limit Test, Shrinkage  Limit Test, Laboratory Permeability Test, Specific Gravity Test, Consolidation  Test.
LIST OF TESTS :
1.
Triaxial Test
2.
Direct Shear Test
3.
Mechanical Analysis (grain size)
4.
Standard Proctor Compaction Test
5.
Atterberg's Limit Test
6.
Shrinkage Limit Test
7.
Laboratory Permeability Test
8.
Specific Gravity Test
9.
Consolidation Test
CONSTRUCTION MATERIALS TESTING LABORATORY
LIST OF TESTS :
A. COARSE AGGREGATE :
Aggregate Impact Value Test, Aggregate Crushing Value Test, Los
Angles
Abrasion Value Test, Specific Gravity Test, Water Absorption
Test, Flakiness
Index Test, Elongation Index Test, Sieving and Grading,
Soundness Test.

Concrete Cube Test

Triaxial Shear Test, Uniaxial Compression Test, Brazillian Test
(Indirect Tensile),
Point Load Test, Physical Properties of Rock Sample.
